title: Kubernetes with Minikube (10/10): Production Readiness
summary: Add health checks (probes), resource limits, and observability to your services — the features that separate a demo from a production-ready system.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (9/10): Ingress and HTTP Routing
summary: Set up an Ingress controller for path-based and host-based HTTP routing, replacing random NodePort numbers with clean URLs.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (8/10): Service-to-Service Communication
summary: Configure services to talk to each other using Kubernetes DNS, ConfigMaps, and NGINX reverse proxying.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (7/10): Multi-Service Architecture
summary: Deploy a second application alongside the first and learn how Kubernetes isolates services using labels, selectors, and independent scaling.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (6/10): Scaling and Self-Healing
summary: Scale your app to multiple replicas, watch Kubernetes automatically replace crashed pods, and see load balancing in action.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (5/10): YAML and Declarative Configuration
summary: Transition from CLI commands to YAML manifests — the critical shift to infrastructure as code and the real way to work with Kubernetes.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (4/10): Exposing Your App
summary: Learn about Services, NodePort, and port-forwarding to make your application accessible from outside the cluster.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (3/10): Your First Deployment
summary: Deploy your first application and learn about Pods and Deployments — the core building blocks of everything you run on Kubernetes.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (2/10): Setting Up Your Local Cluster
summary: Install Minikube and kubectl on macOS, start your first Kubernetes cluster, and explore what's already running inside it.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10): What is Kubernetes?
summary: Build a mental model of containers, orchestration, and Kubernetes architecture before touching any tools — the foundation for the entire series.

title: Hands-on: Setting up a Local Cluster with KinD
summary: A step-by-step guide to running Kubernetes locally using Docker container nodes. Perfect for testing CNI and Ingress controllers.
